canonical_definition: A behavioral marker indicating that a model produces responses consistent with understanding mental states, without implying the presence of an internal causal model or true cognitive mechanism underlying such understanding.
papers: Position: Principles of Animal Cognition to Improve LLM Evaluations
Position: Theory of Mind Benchmarks are Broken for Large Language Models
